title: Australia's Energy Balance: Consumption, Supply and the Petroleum Challenge
summary: A whole-of-system view of Australian energy, connecting generation, sectoral consumption, state variation and petroleum dependence.
key_takeaway: Electricity is central to decarbonisation, but a credible Australian transition must also confront the broader energy balance ? especially petroleum use and the sectors that depend on it.

inline_figures.caption: Sectoral energy use follows different paths, so the transition needs more than a single technology solution.
Petroleum products remain a central part of Australia's energy system, especially for transport-linked services.
